product: Custom Docker Images for AI Runtime CLI
tagline: Enhance your AI workloads with tailored Docker environments.
what_it_does: This feature allows users to bring their own Docker container images to AI Runtime CLI workloads, enabling the use of specific system library versions, complex dependencies, and reproducible environments. Users can register public or private Docker images, which are then cached in the Databricks platform for use in workloads. The registration process ensures that the images are ready for execution within 2-6 minutes.
why_it_matters: This capability empowers data scientists and engineers to customize their environments, leading to improved reproducibility of research results and the ability to leverage specific dependencies that may not be available in standard images. This flexibility can enhance productivity and reduce the time spent on environment setup, ultimately accelerating the development of AI models.
plain_summary: This feature allows users to bring their own Docker container images to AI Runtime CLI workloads, enabling the use of specific system library versions, complex dependencies, and reproducible environments. Users can register public or private Docker images…

capabilities.title: Custom Image Registration
capabilities.desc: Users can register their Docker images with the AI Runtime CLI for use in workloads.

capabilities.title: Support for Public and Private Images
capabilities.desc: Both public and private Docker Hub images can be registered and used.

capabilities.title: Environment Variable Injection
capabilities.desc: AI Runtime injects essential environment variables into containers at runtime.

capabilities.title: Use of Databricks Base Images
capabilities.desc: Users can build custom images based on Databricks-provided base images for optimized performance.

prerequisites: Docker Hub account for private images.
use_cases: Reproducing research results with specific library versions.
Using complex dependencies that do not fit standard configurations.
Leveraging organizationally built images for compliance and security.
Accelerating model development by reducing environment setup time.
limitations: Images must be hosted on Docker Hub; other registries are not supported.
Image size must be under 20 GB.
WORKDIR is not honored; use absolute paths.
environment.dependencies and environment.version cannot be used with environment.docker_image.url.

steps.title: Install AI Runtime CLI
steps.desc: Ensure the AI Runtime CLI is installed on your system.

steps.title: Register Docker Image
steps.desc: Use 'air register image' command to register your Docker image.

steps.title: Specify Image in Workload
steps.desc: Include the Docker image URL in your workload YAML file.

steps.title: Submit Workload
steps.desc: Run your workload using the 'air run' command.

steps.title: Monitor Execution
steps.desc: Check the execution status and logs for your workload.

key_takeaway: Custom Docker images for AI Runtime CLI workloads provide flexibility and control over the execution environment, enhancing productivity and reproducibility.
